metaTitle: Export Table as CSV Online — Free | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Export tables to clean, safe CSV files in one click. Injection-safe sanitisation included. Works with Excel, Google Sheets, and databases. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Export to CSV — clean, safe, and universally compatible.
heroSubtext: One click exports your table as a properly formatted CSV file. Compatible with every spreadsheet, database, data pipeline, and analysis tool that reads structured data.

benefits.heading: Injection-safe sanitisation
benefits.body: CSV injection is a real vulnerability — values starting with =, +, -, or @ can execute formulas when opened in Excel. Tablesmit sanitises every cell value before export, wrapping dangerous characters safely.

benefits.heading: Works with everything
benefits.body: CSV is the universal format for structured data. Every spreadsheet application, database, data warehouse, and analysis tool reads it. If a system can import data, it reads CSV.

benefits.heading: Caption exports as a header comment
benefits.body: If your table has a caption, it exports as the first line of the CSV. Analysis tools that support comments display it as context. Tools that don't simply skip it.

benefits.heading: Processed entirely in your browser
benefits.body: The CSV is generated using PapaParse, entirely client-side. Your data is never uploaded to a server. The file downloads instantly, direct from memory.

steps.heading: Build your table
steps.body: Set up your rows, columns, and data. Column types, themes, and borders are a display layer — CSV export contains the raw values.

steps.heading: Click CSV in the Export panel
steps.body: Find the Export section in the right sidebar. Click CSV. The file downloads immediately.

steps.heading: Open in any tool
steps.body: Open the file in Excel, Google Sheets, Python, R, a database importer — anything that reads CSV. The data is clean and ready.

useCases: Exporting a data table for import into a PostgreSQL or MySQL database
Sharing structured data with a developer or analyst who will process it programmatically
Exporting to CSV for use in Python (pandas), R, or a statistical analysis tool
Creating a clean dataset file from manually entered table data
